balanced.summary: Hard failures or strong warnings remain; fix or ask user before Balanced depth output.

matches: Inputs: Input file: /Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/dev/evals/samples/generated-ai/02-cultural-didion.md ```markdown # Why The year of magical thinking should be reread every decade Joan Didion’s The year of magical thinking is often described as a grief memoir, which is accurate but insufficient.
Its subject is the year after the sudden death of Didion’s husband, John Gregory Dunne, while their daughter, Quintana, was gravely ill.
Not because its meaning changes, exactly, but because the reader’s defences do.
Didion’s account is precise because precision is the only instrument she still trusts.
In early adulthood, Didion’s forensic attention can look like strength.
The title’s “magical thinking” is not ornamental.
This is the book’s most famous example because it is so small and so devastating.
Didion’s great cultural importance lies partly in her refusal to sentimentalise this.
The “vortex effect” is central to this.
Later, the book’s real subject may appear to be marriage: not romance as an event, but intimacy as accumulated reference.
Didion and Dunne’s marriage was also a working partnership, an editorial relationship, a long conversation.
That is one of the book’s sharpest insights.
John had been present for the formation of Didion’s adult life.
Quintana’s illness means Didion is navigating intensive care, diagnoses, specialist language, medical uncertainty and bureaucratic routines at the same time as she is absorbing John’s death.
Didion’s style is inseparable from this argument.
Each decade brings the reader closer to the book’s facts.
Didion’s book waits for the reader at each threshold.
Didion’s achievement is not that she makes grief intelligible.

expectations.guidance: Use as a rhythm signal, not a hard failure for short or intentionally uniform forms.
Fix generic hype; recommend preserving quoted marketing copy or voiced enthusiasm.
Fix inflated importance unless the source genuinely argues significance.
Fix contrived reframes at Balanced and All; recommend preserving only purposeful contrast at Balanced.

not merely background. They expose the limits of expertise. Doctors know things Didion does not know, but
are not merely background. They expose the limits of expertise. Doctors know things Didion does not know, but

Fix at every depth; unfilled placeholders are generated/template residue.
Fix at Balanced and All; these phrases usually mark generated explainer structure rather than content.
Review repeated 'This/That highlights...' starts; replace vague subjects with concrete nouns when the antecedent is unclear.
Fix decorative triads; recommend preserving meaningful lists and deliberate rhetoric.
Fix tacked-on analysis clauses unless they carry precise causal meaning.
Fix atmospheric filler; recommend preserving gothic, literary, or quoted prose.
Fix generic quietness mood; recommend preserving if quietness is the actual subject or voice.
Fix article-template questions; recommend preserving interviews, teaching prose, or deliberate argument.
Fix unnecessary listification; recommend preserving reference, instructional, or interview structure.
Remove decorative symbols in prose; preserve only where symbols are part of a real UI, checklist, or quoted source.
Fix generic turning-point beats; recommend preserving memoir or scene structure when earned.
Fix at Balanced and All; at Balanced, recommend preserving only if it matches the source genre.
Fix generic signposts in prose; recommend preserving academic/instructional structure when useful.

expectations.evidence: Found 1 heading(s): ['# Why The year of magical thinking should be rerea']
expectations.guidance: Fix markdown headings and plain title headings when prose should flow; recommend preserving web articles, guides, and reference docs.
Fix at every depth; recommend preserving only when quoting corporate source language.
Fix mechanical sentence starts; recommend preserving only if rhythmically deliberate.
Fix evasive hedging; recommend preserving scientific qualification and honest uncertainty.
Fix generic reveal structures; recommend preserving deliberate rhetoric only with strong reason.
Review dense negation as a style signal; preserve polemic or technical qualification when purposeful.
matches: Do not edit repository files.
Do not save files.
Do not mention this evaluation harness.
Didion does not stage grief as an emotional revelation.
The title’s “magical thinking” is not ornamental.
It is not an extravagant delusion.
Grief does not always announce itself as despair.
It can appear as a logistical hesitation: keep the shoes, preserve the room, do not cancel the account, do not move the glass.
She does not turn grief into moral improvement.
She does not claim loss made her wiser, kinder, or more authentic.
She is not interested in uplift.
These are not decorative flashbacks.
A place is not just a place.
Grief is not only missing someone.
Didion does not trust it, but she cannot stop depending on it.
The medical scenes are not merely background.
Doctors know things Didion does not know, but they do not know everything she needs to know.
She does not speak for everyone.
Later, it may be the hospital corridor, the medical record, the sudden unreliability of memory, or the terrible fact that ordinary life continues to look ordinary until it does not.
Didion’s achievement is not that she makes grief intelligible.

expectations.guidance: Review as a structural signal; vary paragraph lengths when the piece reads like a generated article template.
Cut generic paragraph-final summaries unless they carry a necessary argument turn.
Fix at Balanced and All for reviews and criticism; replace generic balance with concrete claims from the work.
Review student/assignment prose for mirrored rubric language; preserve only where explicitly analysing a rubric.
Use as a coarse signal; old prose, dialogue, and technical writing may fail legitimately.

expectations.evidence: Found 10 triad(s): ['medical language, social performance, and the collapse of ordinary', 'an anatomy of denial, a critique of competence, and a warning about the', 'visiting Quintana in hospital, Didion and Dunne return to their', 'loss made her wiser, kinder, or more authentic', 'curated vulnerability, the language of healing and closure', 'specialist language, medical uncertainty and bureaucratic routines at the', 'chronic illness, disability systems or end-of-life care', 'The repetitions, returns and abrupt transitions enact the', 'a particular class world, in the medical and literary culture of the', 'manners, memory and routine']
